_meta.document_title: YPIT Consultancies — Internal Operations System: Master Frontend Specification
_meta.usage: This is a complete AI-code-assistant prompt specification. Feed sections phase by phase into Cursor, Claude, or Copilot. Each section is self-contained and references shared design system tokens. Always read the tech_stack and design_system sections before starting any component.
_meta.author_note: Built for YPIT Consultancies, Dar es Salaam, Tanzania. All data is mocked — no real backend exists during frontend development. The system must behave as if fully connected.

project.name: YPIT Consultancies Internal Operations System
project.description: A comprehensive, role-based internal office management system for YPIT Consultancies, a study abroad consultancy headquartered in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania. The system manages the full student lifecycle — from initial lead generation through counseling, payment, university application, visa processing, travel, and post-arrival monitoring — with strict department-level access control, daily task management, and a hierarchical reporting workflow.

company.name: YPIT Consultancies
company.address: NIC Investment House, 4th Floor, Dar es Salaam, Tanzania

framework.name: Next.js 15
framework.router: App Router (app/ directory only — no pages/ directory)

rendering.default: Server Components (RSC) by default for all page-level components
rendering.client_components: Mark with 'use client' directive ONLY when component uses: useState, useEffect, useContext, event handlers, browser APIs, framer-motion animations, recharts, or any interactivity
rendering.server_actions: Use Next.js Server Actions (app/actions/) for all form submissions and data mutations — even though data is mocked, structure actions as if they call a real API
rendering.streaming: Use React Suspense + loading.tsx for all data-fetching components to enable streaming SSR
rendering.error_handling: Use error.tsx at route segment level for error boundaries

special_files.layout.tsx: Root layout and group layouts only — never put page logic here
special_files.loading.tsx: Skeleton loading UI per route segment — always implement these
special_files.error.tsx: Must be a Client Component ('use client') — handles runtime errors per segment
special_files.page.tsx: Default export — Server Component unless interactivity needed
special_files.route.ts: API Route Handlers in app/api/ — used for mock API endpoints

caching.note: Even with mock data, structure fetch calls using Next.js fetch with cache options to prepare for real backend
caching.pattern: fetch(url, { cache: 'no-store' }) for dynamic data, fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 60 } }) for semi-static data

key_features_to_use.use_hook: Use the new React 19 'use()' hook to read promises and context in Server Components where applicable
key_features_to_use.use_form_status: Import { useFormStatus } from 'react-dom' for form submit states instead of manual isLoading state
key_features_to_use.use_action_state: Import { useActionState } from 'react' for form action state management (replaces useFormState from react-dom)
key_features_to_use.use_optimistic: Import { useOptimistic } from 'react' for optimistic UI updates on task status changes, student pipeline stage changes
key_features_to_use.ref_as_prop: In React 19, refs can be passed as regular props — no need for forwardRef wrapper
key_features_to_use.server_components: Async Server Components: export default async function Page() — await data directly at component level
key_features_to_use.transitions: Use useTransition() for non-urgent state updates like filter changes and search to keep UI responsive
key_features_to_use.deferred_value: Use useDeferredValue() for search input filtering to avoid blocking renders

patterns.data_fetching: Fetch mock data in Server Components using async/await directly. Pass data as props to Client Components.
patterns.client_state: Use useState + useReducer for complex local state in Client Components
patterns.global_state: Use React Context + useContext for auth session, notifications count, sidebar state
patterns.forms: Use Server Actions with useActionState for all form submissions
patterns.optimistic_updates: Use useOptimistic for task status drag-and-drop and pipeline stage updates

tailwind.config: tailwind.config.ts — extend theme with custom colors, fonts, shadows, border-radius matching design system tokens
tailwind.css_variables: Define all design tokens as CSS custom properties in globals.css under :root — use var(--color-primary) etc.
tailwind.dark_mode: Not required in v1 — light mode only
tailwind.important_classes: Use cn() utility from clsx + tailwind-merge for conditional class merging — install clsx and tailwind-merge
tailwind.responsive: Mobile-first: sm:, md:, lg:, xl: breakpoints. Sidebar collapses below lg:.

shadcn_ui.version: shadcn/ui (latest — uses Radix UI primitives under the hood)
shadcn_ui.installation: npx shadcn@latest init — choose style: Default, base color: Neutral
shadcn_ui.theming: Override shadcn CSS variables in globals.css to match YPIT brand: --primary: 9c003d values in HSL format

lucide_react.usage: import { IconName } from 'lucide-react' — tree-shakeable, use for all UI icons
lucide_react.size_convention: Default size={18} for nav icons, size={16} for inline text icons, size={20} for action buttons, size={24} for KPI cards

react_icons.usage: Use for brand/social icons not in lucide — e.g. FiGlobe from feather, RiWhatsappLine for WhatsApp contact links
react_icons.import_pattern: import { RiWhatsappLine } from 'react-icons/ri' — always import from specific subpackage for tree-shaking

framer_motion.import: import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'motion/react' — use the new motion/react package for React 19 compatibility
usage_rules: Use motion.div for slide-in panels, modal overlays, page transitions
Use AnimatePresence for mount/unmount animations on panels and toasts
Use layout prop on motion.div for smooth list reorders (task kanban drag)
Keep animations subtle — max 300ms duration for micro-interactions, 400ms for panels
Use spring physics for drag-and-drop: type: 'spring', stiffness: 300, damping: 30

animation_presets.slide_in_right: { initial: { x: '100%', opacity: 0 }, animate: { x: 0, opacity: 1 }, exit: { x: '100%', opacity: 0 }, transition: { type: 'spring', stiffness: 300, damping: 30 } }
animation_presets.fade_up: { initial: { y: 20, opacity: 0 }, animate: { y: 0, opacity: 1 }, transition: { duration: 0.3 } }
animation_presets.scale_in: { initial: { scale: 0.95, opacity: 0 }, animate: { scale: 1, opacity: 1 }, transition: { duration: 0.2 } }

recharts.usage: All charts are Client Components ('use client'). Wrap in ResponsiveContainer for responsive sizing.

chart_types.BarChart: Monthly intake, revenue, lead sources, login activity
chart_types.AreaChart: Revenue trend over time with gradient fill
chart_types.PieChart: Pipeline stage breakdown, visa status, staff by dept

recharts.tooltip_style: Custom tooltip: white bg, #0a0a0a text, subtle shadow, rounded-md, DM Sans font

utilities.date_handling: date-fns latest — formatDistanceToNow, format, isToday, isPast, isFuture for all date display
utilities.class_merging: clsx + tailwind-merge combined in lib/utils.ts as cn() function
utilities.form_validation: zod latest for all form schema validation — define schemas in lib/validations/
utilities.react_hook_form: react-hook-form v7 with @hookform/resolvers/zod — use for all complex forms with validation
utilities.drag_and_drop: @dnd-kit/core + @dnd-kit/sortable for task kanban board drag and drop
utilities.notifications: Use shadcn Toaster (sonner) for success/error toast notifications after every action
utilities.cn_utility: export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) { return twMerge(clsx(inputs)) } — place in lib/utils.ts

rules: No 'any' types — use unknown and narrow properly
All component props must have explicit interface definitions
All mock data arrays must be typed with their corresponding interface
Use discriminated unions for role types and pipeline stage types
Use const enums or as const objects for Role and PipelineStage
All Server Actions must return typed ActionResult objects

path_aliases.@/: root directory alias configured in tsconfig.json paths

fonts.provider: next/font/google — use Next.js built-in font optimization

heading.usage: All headings h1-h4, KPI numbers, navigation labels, page titles, card titles

body.usage: All body text, descriptions, table cells, form labels, button text

fonts.implementation: Import both fonts in app/layout.tsx using next/font/google, apply CSS variables to <html> element, configure in tailwind.config.ts under theme.extend.fontFamily

design_system.description: All design tokens. Define as CSS variables in globals.css and mirror in tailwind.config.ts. Every component must reference these tokens — never hardcode hex values in component files.

primary.usage: Primary buttons, active nav, badges, chart fill, links

primary_muted.usage: Icon backgrounds in KPI cards, hover states, selected rows

primary_muted_strong.usage: Focused input rings, selected item backgrounds

white.usage: Card backgrounds, sidebar contrast, form backgrounds

gray_50.usage: Page background, table row hover, input backgrounds

gray_200.usage: Card borders, table borders, input borders

gray_400.usage: Placeholder text, muted icons, chart axes

success.usage: Payment confirmed, accepted, active, good wellbeing

purple.usage: Travel planning, draft status, special stages

design_system.globals_css_template: In app/globals.css: @import url fonts, define :root { --font-syne, --font-dm-sans, --color-primary: 9c003d (HSL for shadcn), all semantic colors }. Override shadcn --primary, --primary-foreground, --ring to match #9c003d brand. Set body { font-family: var(--font-dm-sans); background: #f9fafb; color: #0a0a0a; }. Set h1-h6 { font-family: var(--font-syne); }.

typescript_types.description: Single source of truth for all TypeScript interfaces and enums used across the project

enums_and_const_objects.Role: export const ROLES = { MANAGING_DIRECTOR: 'MANAGING_DIRECTOR', MARKETING_MANAGER: 'MARKETING_MANAGER', IT_ADMIN: 'IT_ADMIN', FINANCE: 'FINANCE', ADMISSIONS: 'ADMISSIONS', TRAVEL: 'TRAVEL', OPERATIONS: 'OPERATIONS', MARKETING_STAFF: 'MARKETING_STAFF', SUB_AGENT: 'SUB_AGENT' } as const; export type Role = typeof ROLES[keyof typeof ROLES];
enums_and_const_objects.PipelineStage: export const PIPELINE_STAGES = { LEAD: 'LEAD', COUNSELING: 'COUNSELING', PAYMENT_PENDING: 'PAYMENT_PENDING', PAYMENT_CONFIRMED: 'PAYMENT_CONFIRMED', APPLICATION_SUBMITTED: 'APPLICATION_SUBMITTED', UNIVERSITY_ACCEPTED: 'UNIVERSITY_ACCEPTED', TRAVEL_PLANNING: 'TRAVEL_PLANNING', TRAVELLED: 'TRAVELLED', MONITORING: 'MONITORING' } as const; export type PipelineStage = typeof PIPELINE_STAGES[keyof typeof PIPELINE_STAGES];

interfaces.User: { id: string; fullName: string; email: string; role: Role; department: string; avatar?: string; phone?: string; status: 'ACTIVE' | 'INACTIVE' | 'SUSPENDED'; lastLogin?: string; createdAt: string; createdBy?: string; }
interfaces.Session: { userId: string; fullName: string; email: string; role: Role; department: string; avatar?: string; }
interfaces.Student: { id: string; fullName: string; avatar?: string; registrationNumber: string; nationality: string; passportNumber?: string; gender: 'MALE' | 'FEMALE' | 'OTHER'; dateOfBirth: string; age: number; email: string; phone: string; whatsapp?: string; targetUniversity: string; targetCountry: string; targetProgram: string; targetIntake: string; pipelineStage: PipelineStage; leadSource: LeadSource; assignedAgentId?: string; assignedAgentName?: string; marketingStaffId?: string; createdAt: string; updatedAt: string; notes?: string; }
interfaces.Lead: { id: string; fullName: string; phone: string; email?: string; source: LeadSource; interestedIn: string; interestedCountry?: string; status: 'NEW' | 'CONTACTED' | 'COUNSELED' | 'CONVERTED' | 'LOST'; assignedToId?: string; assignedToName?: string; createdAt: string; updatedAt: string; notes?: string; followUpDate?: string; convertedStudentId?: string; }
interfaces.PaymentRecord: { id: string; studentId: string; studentName: string; agencyFee: number; agencyFeePaid: number; agencyFeeDate?: string; applicationFee: number; applicationFeePaid: number; applicationFeeDate?: string; tuitionFee: number; tuitionFeePaid: number; tuitionFeeDate?: string; hostelFee: number; hostelFeePaid: number; hostelFeeDate?: string; totalDue: number; totalPaid: number; balance: number; status: PaymentStatus; currency: string; receiptNumbers: string[]; lastPaymentDate?: string; recordedBy?: string; notes?: string; }
interfaces.Application: { id: string; studentId: string; studentName: string; university: string; country: string; program: string; level: 'UNDERGRADUATE' | 'POSTGRADUATE' | 'PHD' | 'DIPLOMA' | 'CERTIFICATE'; intake: string; submissionDate?: string; status: ApplicationStatus; offerLetterUrl?: string; conditionsUrl?: string; decisionDate?: string; decisionNotes?: string; submittedBy?: string; updatedAt: string; }
interfaces.Document: { id: string; studentId: string; type: 'PASSPORT' | 'TRANSCRIPT' | 'CERTIFICATE' | 'OFFER_LETTER' | 'VISA' | 'BANK_STATEMENT' | 'PHOTO' | 'REFERENCE_LETTER' | 'OTHER'; name: string; url: string; uploadedAt: string; uploadedBy: string; verified: boolean; notes?: string; }
interfaces.TravelRecord: { id: string; studentId: string; studentName: string; passportStatus: PassportStatus; passportNumber?: string; passportExpiry?: string; visaStatus: VisaStatus; visaType?: string; visaApplicationDate?: string; visaAppointmentDate?: string; visaApprovalDate?: string; visaExpiryDate?: string; flightDate?: string; flightNumber?: string; airline?: string; departureCity: string; destinationCity: string; destinationAirport?: string; airportPickupArranged: boolean; pickupContactName?: string; pickupContactPhone?: string; accommodationAddress?: string; travelStatus: TravelStatus; updatedAt: string; }
interfaces.OperationsRecord: { id: string; studentId: string; studentName: string; university: string; country: string; arrivalDate?: string; enrollmentConfirmed: boolean; enrollmentDate?: string; studentIdNumber?: string; accommodationAddress?: string; localContactName?: string; localContactPhone?: string; wellbeingStatus: WellbeingStatus; lastCheckIn?: string; checkInCount: number; escalationFlag: boolean; escalationReason?: string; notes?: string; }
interfaces.CheckIn: { id: string; studentId: string; studentName: string; wellbeingStatus: WellbeingStatus; notes: string; submittedById: string; submittedByName: string; createdAt: string; }
interfaces.Task: { id: string; title: string; description: string; assignedToIds: string[]; assignedToNames: string[]; assignedById: string; assignedByName: string; department: string; priority: TaskPriority; status: TaskStatus; dueDate: string; createdAt: string; updatedAt: string; tags: string[]; endOfDayReport?: EndOfDayReport; attachmentUrls?: string[]; }
interfaces.EndOfDayReport: { taskSummary: string; progressMade: string; blockers?: string; tomorrowPlan: string; percentageComplete: number; submittedAt: string; submittedById: string; submittedByName: string; acknowledgedAt?: string; acknowledgedById?: string; }
interfaces.Report: { id: string; title: string; period: ReportPeriod; periodStart?: string; periodEnd?: string; submittedById: string; submittedByName: string; department: string; submittedToId?: string; submittedToName: string; submittedToRole: Role; summary: string; keyMetrics?: Record<string, string | number>; status: 'DRAFT' | 'SUBMITTED' | 'ACKNOWLEDGED' | 'ARCHIVED'; createdAt: string; acknowledgedAt?: string; }
interfaces.AuditLog: { id: string; userId: string; userName: string; userRole: Role; action: 'LOGIN' | 'LOGOUT' | 'CREATE' | 'UPDATE' | 'DELETE' | 'PASSWORD_RESET' | 'ROLE_CHANGE' | 'STAGE_CHANGE' | 'PAYMENT_RECORDED' | 'REPORT_SUBMITTED' | 'TASK_ASSIGNED'; module: string; detail: string; entityId?: string; entityType?: string; previousValue?: string; newValue?: string; ipAddress?: string; userAgent?: string; timestamp: string; }
interfaces.Notification: { id: string; userId: string; title: string; message: string; type: NotificationType; read: boolean; link?: string; entityId?: string; createdAt: string; }
interfaces.KPIMetric: { label: string; value: string | number; icon: string; trend?: string; trendDirection?: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral'; trendColor?: string; }
interfaces.ActionResult: { success: boolean; message: string; data?: unknown; errors?: Record<string, string[]>; }

mock_data_layer.description: All mock data files. Import these in Server Component pages to simulate database queries. Each file exports a typed array and optionally helper functions (findById, filterBy, etc.).

mockUsers.ts.description: 9 mock users — one per role. Used for authentication simulation.

mockStudents.ts.description: 25 students spread across all 9 pipeline stages for rich testing of all views
mockStudents.ts.fields_to_populate: All fields in Student interface. Use pravatar.cc for avatars. Registration numbers: YP-2026-001 through YP-2026-025. Mix nationalities: Tanzanian, Kenyan, Ugandan, Rwandan, Ethiopian. Target universities: mix of UK, Malaysia, China, Australia, India institutions.

mockPayments.ts.description: Payment records for all students in PAYMENT_PENDING stage and beyond
mockPayments.ts.currency_note: Use TZS (Tanzanian Shilling) as currency. Agency fee: 500,000–1,500,000 TZS. Application fee: 200,000–800,000 TZS. Tuition: 5,000,000–25,000,000 TZS. Hostel: 2,000,000–8,000,000 TZS.

mockApplications.ts.description: University application records for students in APPLICATION_SUBMITTED stage and beyond

mockDocuments.ts.description: Document records per student. Use placeholder URLs.

mockTravel.ts.description: Travel records for students in TRAVEL_PLANNING and TRAVELLED stages

mockOperations.ts.description: Operations monitoring records for TRAVELLED and MONITORING students

mockTasks.ts.description: 40 tasks spread across all departments, priorities, statuses. Some have end-of-day reports already submitted.

mockAuditLogs.ts.description: 100 audit log entries of various types

mockNotifications.ts.description: Notifications per user — mix of read/unread

mock_data_layer.helper_pattern: Each mock file should also export a getMock[Entity]Stats() function that returns aggregated numbers for KPI cards — e.g. getMockStudentStats() returns { total, byStage, thisMonth, lastMonth, trend }
mock_data_layer.delay_simulation: Export a simulateDelay(ms = 300) async utility in lib/mock/utils.ts — call await simulateDelay() in Server Components to simulate network latency during development

auth_system.description: Mock auth using sessionStorage (not localStorage — sessionStorage clears on tab close, more secure). Wrap with a React Context for session access across Client Components. Server Components read a mock cookie.

exports: getSession(): Session | null — reads from sessionStorage on client, mock cookie on server
setSession(user: User): void — writes to sessionStorage + sets document.cookie mock
clearSession(): void — clears sessionStorage + cookie, redirects to /login
hasRole(session: Session, roles: Role[]): boolean — checks if session role is in allowed roles
getRedirectPath(role: Role): string — returns default route per role

hooks/useSession.ts.description: Client hook to access session from Context
hooks/useSession.ts.signature: export function useSession(): { session: Session | null; isLoading: boolean }

contexts/SessionContext.tsx.description: 'use client' context provider — wraps the dashboard layout, reads sessionStorage, provides session to all client components
contexts/SessionContext.tsx.pattern: const SessionContext = createContext<SessionContextValue>(null); export function SessionProvider({ children }) { const [session, setSession] = useState<Session | null>(null); useEffect(() => { const s = sessionStorage.getItem('ypit_session'); if (s) setSession(JSON.parse(s)); }, []); return <SessionContext.Provider value={{ session }}>{children}</SessionContext.Provider> }

middleware.ts.description: Next.js middleware for route protection — checks for mock session cookie
middleware.ts.pattern: export function middleware(request: NextRequest) { const session = request.cookies.get('ypit_session'); if (!session && !request.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith('/login')) { return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', request.url)); } return NextResponse.next(); } export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!api|_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)'] };

login_flow: 1. User submits email + password on /login page
3. Server Action matches against mockUsers, returns ActionResult
4. On success: client stores session in sessionStorage + mock cookie, redirects to getRedirectPath(role)
5. On failure: useActionState displays error message inline

login.layout: Full-screen split — 45% left brand panel, 55% right form panel. No sidebar. No topbar.

content: YPIT logo SVG white at top-left with padding
Large decorative geometric pattern: overlapping circles/rings in white at 8% opacity as background texture
Center content: large white Syne heading 'Empowering Students, Fulfilling Dreams' in 3xl bold
Subtitle: 'Your complete study abroad management platform' in white/80% DM Sans
Bottom: three small stat pills in white/10% bg — '500+ Students Placed', '15+ Countries', '98% Visa Success Rate'
YPIT tagline 'Fulfill Your Joy' at very bottom in white/60%
Top-right: 'YPIT OPS v1.0' text in xs muted
Center vertically: heading 'Welcome Back' in Syne 2xl bold text-gray-900
Subheading: 'Sign in to your YPIT workspace' in sm text-gray-500
Form with react-hook-form + zod validation:
Email field: label 'Email Address', Input with Mail lucide icon prefix, placeholder 'you@ypit.com'
Password field: label 'Password', Input with Lock icon prefix, Eye/EyeOff toggle suffix
Checkbox row: 'Remember me' left, 'Forgot password?' link right
Submit button: full width, bg #9c003d, white text 'Sign In', loading spinner via useFormStatus
Error alert: shadcn Alert with AlertCircle icon, red border, appears on invalid credentials
Demo credentials helper: small collapsible section 'Demo Accounts' showing all 9 role credentials in a clean table — helps development testing
Bottom: 'Need help? Contact IT: it@ypit.com'

login.animations: Right panel form fades up on mount with motion/react fade_up preset. Error alert uses AnimatePresence scale_in.

shell_layout.type: Server Component (reads session from cookie for initial render) + Client Components for interactive sidebar parts

sidebar.type: 'use client' — uses useSession, usePathname for active state
sidebar.width: 260px fixed left panel, full height, overflow-y-auto with custom scrollbar
structure: Header section: YPIT logo + wordmark (white) + tagline in xs muted
User card: avatar (shadcn Avatar), full name in sm white font-medium, role badge (RoleBadge component) — separated by subtle gray-800 border
Nav section label: 'MAIN MENU' in 10px uppercase tracking-widest text-gray-500 mt-6 mb-2

Nav items: map over role-specific nav array. Each item: <Link href> with flex items-center gap-3 px-3 py-2 rounded-md. Active: bg #9c003d text white. Hover: bg #1a1a1a. Icon left in size 18. Label in sm DM Sans. Optional unread badge right (red dot with count).
Tools section label: 'SETTINGS' in same style as MAIN MENU
Settings items: Profile Settings, System (IT only)
Bottom pinned: logout button — flex items-center gap-2 text-gray-400 hover:text-white hover:bg-gray-800 px-3 py-2 rounded-md. LogOut icon + 'Logout' label. Calls clearSession() on click.
sidebar.mobile_behavior: Below lg breakpoint: sidebar hidden, hamburger menu in topbar opens mobile drawer using shadcn Sheet from left
sidebar.collapse_behavior: Desktop: sidebar can be collapsed to 72px icon-only mode via toggle button. Store collapsed state in localStorage. When collapsed: hide labels, show only icons with shadcn Tooltip on hover.

structure: Left: hamburger MenuIcon button (mobile only, lg:hidden) + page breadcrumb using shadcn Breadcrumb component — shows current page path

Center: global search bar — shadcn Command dialog triggered by clicking a fake input showing 'Search students, leads...' with Cmd+K shortcut hint. Command dialog searches across students, leads, staff.

Right cluster: notification bell (BellIcon) with unread count badge in #9c003d — opens NotificationPanel Sheet. Separator. User avatar dropdown (shadcn DropdownMenu): shows name, role, links to Profile, Settings, and Logout.

shell_layout.main_content: flex-1 overflow-y-auto bg-gray-50 p-6 (p-4 on mobile)

content: Header: 'Notifications' h3 + 'Mark all as read' button in text-primary
Filter tabs: All | Unread — using simple state toggle
Notification list: map over user notifications sorted by createdAt desc

Each notification: flex row — icon left (type-based, in colored circle), middle (title font-medium, message text-sm text-gray-500, time-ago text-xs text-gray-400), unread indicator (3px left border in #9c003d on unread items)
On click: mark as read, navigate to link if provided
Empty state: Bell icon, 'No notifications yet'

dashboard.type: Server Component — fetch mock data, pass to Client Component children
dashboard.description: Role-aware dashboard. Server Component detects role from session cookie, fetches appropriate mock stats, renders role-specific layout. All chart sub-components are Client Components.

KPICard.props: { label: string; value: string | number; icon: LucideIcon; trend?: string; trendDirection?: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral'; description?: string; }
KPICard.design: White card bg-white rounded-xl shadow-card p-5. Top row: label text-sm text-gray-500 DM Sans + icon in 40px rounded-lg bg-primary-muted flex items-center justify-center. Value: text-4xl font-bold Syne text-gray-900 mt-3. Trend row: ArrowUp/ArrowDown icon (green/red) + trend text text-sm. Optional description text-xs text-gray-400. On hover: shadow-card-hover transition-shadow 200ms.

MANAGING_DIRECTOR.greeting: 'Welcome back, {name}' in Syne h1 + date string
MANAGING_DIRECTOR.kpi_row: 4 KPI cards: Total Students (Users icon), Active Leads (Target icon), Revenue Collected TZS (DollarSign icon), Students Travelled (Plane icon)
MANAGING_DIRECTOR.row_2: Left 2/3: Monthly intake bar chart (BarChart — Jan-Dec, color #9c003d). Right 1/3: Pipeline stage donut chart (PieChart with legend below).
MANAGING_DIRECTOR.row_3: Left 1/2: Recent activity feed (last 10 events across all departments — ActivityFeed component). Right 1/2: Department report status table (each dept, last report date, status badge, View link).
MANAGING_DIRECTOR.row_4: Revenue area chart spanning full width — monthly TZS collected with gradient fill #9c003d → transparent.

MARKETING_MANAGER.kpi_row: New Leads This Month, Conversion Rate %, Students in Pipeline, Pending Dept Reports
MARKETING_MANAGER.row_2: Lead source bar chart + Lead status Kanban summary (count per status column)
MARKETING_MANAGER.row_3: Team tasks completion today (progress bars per staff member) + Recent dept reports received

IT_ADMIN.kpi_row: Total Staff Accounts, Active Today, Audit Events Today, Inactive Accounts
IT_ADMIN.row_2: Login activity bar chart (last 7 days) + Staff by department donut chart
IT_ADMIN.row_3: Recent audit log table (last 10 entries) — same style as Image 2 table

FINANCE.kpi_row: Total Collected TZS, Pending Payments count, Overdue count, Students Fully Cleared
FINANCE.row_2: Monthly collections bar chart + Fee type stacked bar (agency/application/tuition/hostel)
FINANCE.row_3: Recent payments table + Outstanding balances list

ADMISSIONS.kpi_row: Applications Submitted, Pending Decisions, Accepted, Rejected
ADMISSIONS.row_2: Application status donut + Applications by country bar chart
ADMISSIONS.row_3: Recent applications table + Documents completion checklist summary

TRAVEL.kpi_row: Awaiting Travel, Visas Approved, Travelled This Month, Pickups Arranged
TRAVEL.row_2: Upcoming departures timeline list (next 14 days, card per student with days countdown badge) + Visa status donut
TRAVEL.row_3: Travel tracking list inspired by Image 6 — each student card with step progress (Passport → Visa → Booked → Travelled)

OPERATIONS.kpi_row: Students Abroad, Check-ins This Week, Escalations, Enrolled & Settled
OPERATIONS.row_2: Wellbeing status donut + Check-in frequency bar chart (last 30 days)
OPERATIONS.row_3: Student monitoring list with wellbeing badges

MARKETING_STAFF.kpi_row: My Leads, Converted, My Tasks Today, Reports Pending
MARKETING_STAFF.row_2: My leads pipeline status list + My tasks card grid (compact, last 4)

SUB_AGENT.kpi_row: My Referrals, Converted, Commission Earned TZS, Pending Commission TZS
SUB_AGENT.row_2: My referrals table with status + Commission breakdown card

components: Page header: title 'Students' h1 Syne + breadcrumb + 'Add Student' button (shown only to MARKETING_MANAGER, MANAGING_DIRECTOR) in #9c003d

Filter bar (Client Component 'StudentFilters.tsx'): search Input (useDeferredValue for performance), PipelineStage Select dropdown (all stages + 'All'), Country Select, Gender Select, Date range picker (shadcn Popover + Calendar), Clear filters button
Stats strip above table: small chips showing count per pipeline stage with color dots — e.g. '4 Leads • 3 Counseling • 2 Payment Pending...'
Data table (Client Component 'StudentsTable.tsx'): uses @tanstack/react-table (install it) for sorting, filtering, pagination

Table columns: Checkbox (selectable), Avatar+Name (Avatar component + full name + reg number in smaller muted text below), Nationality, Target University, Target Country, Pipeline Stage (StatusBadge), Assigned Agent, Created Date, Actions (View ChevronRight link + shadcn DropdownMenu for more actions)
Row click navigates to /students/[id]
Pagination: shadcn Pagination component — shows page X of Y, items per page selector (10, 25, 50)
Bulk actions bar: appears when rows selected — 'Export Selected', 'Change Stage' (manager only)
Empty state: EmptyState component with Users icon

detail_page.type: Server Component for data fetch, Client Component child for tabs
components: Back button: ArrowLeft icon + 'Back to Students' — uses router.back()
Student header: Avatar (large 64px), Full Name h1 Syne, Registration number badge, Pipeline stage badge, 'Edit' button (role-restricted)
Pipeline progress bar: PipelineStageBar component showing all 9 stages with current highlighted
Tabs (shadcn Tabs, role-restricted tabs hidden per access_rules):

Tab 'Personal Info': two-column grid of labeled field-value pairs. Edit button opens inline edit mode with react-hook-form. Fields: full name, DOB, gender, nationality, email, phone, whatsapp, passport number.

Tab 'Payments': PaymentBreakdown component — 4 rows (Agency, Application, Tuition, Hostel) each with amount due, amount paid, balance, status badge, payment date. Total summary card at bottom. Record Payment button (Finance only) opens SlideInPanel with payment form.
Tab 'Application': ApplicationCard showing university, country, program, level, intake, submission date, status stepper, offer letter download. Update status button (Admissions only).
Tab 'Travel': TravelCard showing passport status, visa status with substeps, flight details, accommodation, pickup info. Update button (Travel only).
Tab 'Documents': DocumentsList — shadcn Table with type, name, uploaded date, uploaded by, verified badge, download button. Upload button opens file input.
Tab 'Activity Log': ActivityTimeline — reverse-chronological list of all changes to this student, with actor, action, field changed, old→new value, timestamp.
Page header: 'Leads' h1 + 'Add Lead' button
View toggle: Button group — 'Board' (LayoutGrid icon) / 'Table' (List icon) — persists to localStorage

Board view (Kanban): 4 columns — New, Contacted, Counseled, Converted. Each column has header with title + count badge. Cards use @dnd-kit for drag-to-change-status using useOptimistic for instant UI feedback.

Lead card: white bg rounded-lg shadow-card p-4. Full name bold Syne. Phone + source badge row. Interested in text-sm muted. Bottom: assigned agent avatar + name + created date. Right-click or kebab menu: View, Edit, Convert to Student, Mark as Lost.
Table view: same style as Image 1 — columns: Name, Phone, Source badge, Interested In, Status badge, Assigned To, Created Date, Actions

Add Lead SlideInPanel: react-hook-form with zod. Fields: Full Name*, Phone*, Email, Source (Select), Interested In (Input), Interested Country (Select), Notes (Textarea), Assign To (Select — filtered by marketing staff), Follow Up Date (DatePicker). Submit via Server Action.
Convert to Student dialog: confirmation with student registration number preview. On confirm: creates student record, marks lead as CONVERTED.
MARKETING_STAFF and SUB_AGENT: filter applied — only see their own leads (by assignedToId matching session.userId)
Page header: 'Payments & Finance' + 'Submit Report' button (Finance only)
KPI strip: 4 inline stat cards — Total Collected TZS, Pending, Overdue, Fully Cleared students
Filter bar: search by student name/receipt, status filter, date range, currency

Data table inspired by Image 2: columns — Date, Student Name, Receipt Number(s), Agency Fee (paid/due inline), Application Fee (paid/due), Tuition (paid/due), Hostel (paid/due), Total Due, Total Paid, Balance, Status badge (styled exactly like Image 2 pills — Cleared=gray, Partial=green italic, Overdue=orange, Pending=gray), kebab menu
Kebab menu actions: View Full Record, Record New Payment, Generate Receipt PDF, Flag as Overdue, Add Note

Record Payment SlideInPanel: student name (read-only), payment type (Select — agency/application/tuition/hostel), amount (Input with TZS prefix), receipt number (Input), payment date (DatePicker), notes (Textarea), upload receipt image (file input). Submit via Server Action.
Generate Receipt: mock PDF generation — opens a print-friendly modal showing receipt layout
Export: shadcn DropdownMenu — 'Export CSV' / 'Export PDF Report'
Submit Finance Report: opens modal — period select, summary textarea, submits to MD directly

applications.type: Server Component + Client Component for filters and panel
components: Page header: 'University Applications' + 'Submit Report' button (Admissions only)
Filter bar: search, status filter (all ApplicationStatus values), country filter
Status summary chips above table: count per ApplicationStatus with color dots
Applications table: columns — Student (avatar + name), University, Country, Program, Level badge, Intake, Submitted Date, Status badge, Decision Date, Actions
Status badges: Preparing=gray, Submitted=blue, Under Review=yellow, Accepted=green bold, Rejected=red, Waitlisted=purple, Deferred=orange
Row click opens ApplicationDetailPanel (SlideInPanel):
Panel header: student name + current status badge
Application status stepper: horizontal steps Preparing → Submitted → Under Review → Decision (Accepted/Rejected/Waitlisted)
Fields: university, country, program, level, intake, submission date, decision date, notes
Documents section: offer letter upload/view, conditions doc upload/view
Update Status button (Admissions only): opens inner dialog to change status with notes
'Mark as Accepted' special button: triggers confirm dialog, on confirm — changes student pipeline stage to UNIVERSITY_ACCEPTED, sends mock notification to Travel dept
Admissions report submit: period, summary, auto-submits to Marketing Manager
Page header: 'Travel Management' + tab nav: 'All Students' / 'Upcoming Departures' / 'Visa Tracker' (or use sub-routes)
KPI strip: Awaiting Departure, Visas Approved, Travelled This Month, Pickups Arranged

Upcoming departures highlight: horizontal scrollable row of departure cards — each card: student avatar + name, destination flag emoji + city, flight date, days until departure countdown badge (red if <7 days, yellow if <14, green otherwise)
Main table: columns — Student, Destination, Passport Status badge, Visa Status badge, Flight Date, Airline, Pickup Arranged (Yes/No chip), Travel Status badge, Actions
Travel status badges: Planning=gray, Visa Pending=yellow, Ready=green, Travelled=#9c003d white text
Row click opens TravelDetailPanel:
Section 1 — Passport: status badge, passport number, expiry date
Section 2 — Visa: status with visual progress steps (Not Started → Gathering Docs → Applied → Appointment → Decision), visa type, dates
Section 3 — Flight: date, flight number, airline, departure city, destination airport
Section 4 — Accommodation: address, contact person
Section 5 — Pickup: arranged toggle, contact name + phone
Bottom: Update button (Travel staff only), 'Mark as Travelled' button
Travel tracking cards inspired by Image 6: each student shown as a tracking card with step-by-step progress from Passport → Visa → Flight Booked → Travelled with timestamp per completed step

visa_page.description: Dedicated visa tracking sub-page with all visa applications, appointment calendar, and status management

components: Page header: 'Student Monitoring'
KPI strip: Students Abroad, Check-ins This Week, Escalations (highlighted in red if > 0), Enrolled & Settled
Alert banner: if any escalations exist, show red Alert banner at top with student names + quick links

Monitoring table: columns — Student (avatar + name), University, Country, Arrival Date, Last Check-in date, Check-in Count, Wellbeing Status badge, Enrollment Confirmed (toggle chip), Escalation Flag (flame icon if true), Actions
Wellbeing badges: Good=green, Needs Attention=yellow pulsing, Escalated=red with AlertTriangle icon
Row click opens StudentMonitoringPanel: full monitoring record + check-in history timeline
Add Check-in button: SlideInPanel — select student (searchable), wellbeing status (radio group with color indicators), notes textarea (required), submit
Check-ins page /checkins: reverse-chronological activity feed — student avatar, name, wellbeing status dot, notes excerpt, submitted by, time ago. Inspired by Image 6 Track's Actions panel.
Operations report submit: to Marketing Manager
Page header: 'Staff Management' + 'Add Staff Member' button
Stats row: Total, Active (green), Inactive (gray), Suspended (red) — small stat chips
Filter bar: search by name/email, filter by Role (Select), filter by Department, filter by Status

Staff table matching Image 1 style: columns — Avatar+Name+Email, Role badge (RoleBadge component with color per role), Department, Phone, Status badge, Last Login (formatDistanceToNow), Created By, Actions
Row actions (shadcn DropdownMenu — kebab icon): View Profile, Edit Role, Reset Password, Deactivate, Suspend

Add Staff SlideInPanel: Full Name*, Email*, Temporary Password* (auto-generate button), Role* (Select — IT_ADMIN cannot show MANAGING_DIRECTOR or MARKETING_MANAGER options), Department (auto-fills from role selection), Phone, Profile Photo (file upload optional). Submit via Server Action.
Edit Role: dialog — current role shown, new role Select, reason textarea, confirm. MD only can assign manager roles.
Reset Password: dialog — shows newly generated temp password in a copy-to-clipboard box, instructions to share securely
Deactivate/Suspend: confirm dialog with reason textarea
RoleBadge component: shadcn Badge with custom bg/text colors per role from design_system.colors.role_badge_colors
IT_ADMIN restriction: role Select options exclude MANAGING_DIRECTOR and MARKETING_MANAGER. Server Action also validates this server-side.

task_manager.type: Client Component heavy — real-time-like feel with useOptimistic
task_manager.description: The task management system is a critical feature. Every staff member uses it daily. At end of day, each person submits a report on each assigned task. Reports flow up to department heads.
components: Page header: 'Task Manager' + 'Create Task' button (shown to all — staff create their own tasks, managers assign to others)

Tab navigation (shadcn Tabs): 'My Tasks' (tasks assigned to me) | 'Assigned by Me' (tasks I created for others) | 'Department' (all dept tasks — managers see this) | 'Company' (MANAGING_DIRECTOR only)
View toggle per tab: Card Grid (default — 3 col desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile) / Kanban Board (4 columns) / Table
Filter bar: Priority filter (All/High/Medium/Low/Urgent), Status filter, Date range, Search by title
CARD GRID VIEW: TaskCard component exactly matching Image 3 style:
Priority badge top-left: pill shape — High=blue bg blue text, Medium=amber bg amber text, Low=rose bg rose text, Urgent=red bg white text
Status dot top-right: colored dot — TODO=gray, IN_PROGRESS=blue pulsing, COMPLETED=green, BLOCKED=red, REPORTED=purple
Title: Syne font-semibold text-gray-900 mt-3 text-lg — 2 lines max
Description: DM Sans text-sm text-gray-500 mt-1 line-clamp-3
Tags row: small gray pills for each tag
Bottom row: left — calendar icon + due date (red text if past due), right — AvatarCluster of assignees
Report Pending badge: if task is IN_PROGRESS or TODO and it's past 5PM today, show red 'Report Due' badge at bottom
Card hover: shadow elevates, thin 3px left border appears in priority color
Card click: opens TaskDetailPanel

KANBAN VIEW: 4 columns (TODO, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, BLOCKED) using @dnd-kit. Drag cards between columns. useOptimistic for instant UI. Column headers show count. Column backgrounds: TODO=gray-50, IN_PROGRESS=blue-50, COMPLETED=green-50, BLOCKED=red-50.
TABLE VIEW: columns — Title, Priority badge, Status badge, Assigned To (AvatarCluster), Due Date, Report Status, Actions
CREATE TASK SlideInPanel: react-hook-form + zod
Title* (Input)
Description* (Textarea)
Assign To* (multi-select — Command component with search — shows staff filtered by dept for staff, all staff for managers)
Priority* (RadioGroup with colored labels)
Due Date* (DatePicker)
Tags (Input — comma separated, renders as chips)
Submit via Server Action createTask()
TASK DETAIL PANEL (SlideInPanel wide=600px):
Header: task title + status badge + priority badge + kebab menu (Edit, Delete, Mark Complete, Mark Blocked)
Meta row: Assigned by avatar+name | Created date | Due date with countdown
Assignees: AvatarCluster with names list below
Description: full text
Tags: chip pills
Attachments section (if any)
Divider: 'End-of-Day Report' section heading with clock icon
If report already submitted: read-only report card with submitted timestamp, percent complete, all report fields displayed
If report NOT submitted and user is an assignee: END-OF-DAY REPORT FORM:
Progress slider (0-100%) — visual percentage complete indicator
'Task Summary Today' Textarea* — what did you work on?
'Progress Made' Textarea* — what was accomplished?
'Blockers' Textarea — anything blocking progress? (optional)
'Plan for Tomorrow' Textarea* — what will you do next?
'Submit End-of-Day Report' button in #9c003d — disabled until all required fields filled
On submit: task status changes to REPORTED, notification sent to task creator, toast success
If user is task creator viewing someone else's report: see submitted report read-only + 'Acknowledge' button
eod_report_rules: Staff must submit report on every IN_PROGRESS or TODO task assigned to them
Reports flow to: MARKETING_STAFF/ADMISSIONS/TRAVEL/OPERATIONS → Marketing Manager. FINANCE → Managing Director. IT_ADMIN → Managing Director.
After 5PM daily, any task without a submitted report shows a 'Report Due' badge
Marketing Manager and MD see a daily summary widget on their dashboard: 'X of Y staff have submitted today's reports'

components: Page header: 'Reports' + 'Submit New Report' button
Filter tabs: All | This Week | This Month | By Department (manager/MD only)
Reports table: Title, Submitted By (avatar+name), Department badge, Period, Submitted To, Status badge (Submitted=blue, Acknowledged=green, Archived=gray), Date, Actions
Row click opens report detail modal: full summary text, key metrics (if any), submitted by, period, submitted to, acknowledge button

Submit Report SlideInPanel: react-hook-form — Title*, Period (Select: Daily/Weekly/Monthly/Custom), Date Range (if Custom), Summary* (Textarea large), Key Metrics (repeatable key-value pairs with + Add Metric button). Submit To auto-populates based on session role. Submit via Server Action.
Access rules: each role can only submit reports. Cannot see other departments' reports except managers/MD.
Page header: 'Audit Logs'
Filter bar: search by user name, action type Select (all AuditLog action values), module Select, date range

Large table: columns — Timestamp (full datetime), User (avatar+name), Role badge, Action (colored badge), Module, Detail (truncated with expand), IP Address. Color-coded action badges: LOGIN=green, LOGOUT=gray, CREATE=blue, UPDATE=yellow, DELETE=red, PASSWORD_RESET=orange, STAGE_CHANGE=purple.
Row click: expand detail row showing full detail, previous value → new value if applicable
Export CSV button top right
Pagination: 50 per page default

settings.access_roles: ALL (each user sees their own profile settings; IT_ADMIN and MD see additional system settings)
components: Tab navigation: 'My Profile' | 'Security' | 'Notifications' | 'System' (IT_ADMIN + MD only)
My Profile tab: avatar upload (circular dropzone), full name input, phone input, email (read-only), department (read-only), role (read-only). Save button.
Security tab: change password form (current password, new password, confirm). Password strength indicator.
Notifications tab: toggles for each notification type — email notification (mock), in-app notification.
System tab (IT/MD only): system name, company logo upload, default currency, default timezone, support email.

shared_components.description: Reusable components used across multiple pages. All props must be fully typed.

KPICard.props: { label: string; value: string | number; icon: React.ElementType; trend?: string; trendDirection?: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral'; description?: string; className?: string; }
KPICard.design: White bg, rounded-xl, shadow-card, p-5. Icon in 40px square rounded-lg bg-primary-muted. Value in Syne text-3xl font-extrabold. Trend row with ArrowTrendingUp/Down (lucide) colored green/red. Hover: shadow-card-hover.

StatusBadge.design: Uses cn() to apply correct bg/text colors from design_system color maps. Pill shape: px-2.5 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium inline-flex items-center gap-1.

DataTable.props: { columns: ColumnDef<TData>[]; data: TData[]; searchKey?: string; pagination?: boolean; selectable?: boolean; onRowClick?: (row: TData) => void; emptyMessage?: string; }
DataTable.design: shadcn Table base. Header: text-xs uppercase tracking-wide text-gray-500 font-medium bg-gray-50. Rows: border-b border-gray-100 hover:bg-gray-50 cursor-pointer. Selected row: bg-primary-muted. Avatar+name column: flex items-center gap-3 — Avatar (32px) + name font-medium + secondary text below in xs muted.

TaskCard.props: { task: Task; onClick: (task: Task) => void; isDragging?: boolean; }
TaskCard.design: Matches Image 3 exactly. White bg rounded-xl shadow-card p-4 cursor-pointer. Priority badge top-left pill. Status dot top-right. Title Syne font-semibold text-gray-900. Description text-sm text-gray-500 line-clamp-3 mt-1. Tags row text-xs chips mt-2. Bottom flex: calendar+date left, AvatarCluster right. Report Due badge: red text-xs font-medium 'Report Due' with Clock icon. Card border-l-3 on hover in priority color. Framer motion whileHover scale 1.01.

SlideInPanel.props: { open: boolean; onClose: () => void; title: string; subtitle?: string; children: React.ReactNode; width?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl'; footer?: React.ReactNode; }
SlideInPanel.design: shadcn Sheet side='right'. Width variants: sm=380px md=480px lg=600px xl=720px. Header: title Syne h3 + subtitle text-sm muted + X button. Body: ScrollArea flex-1 p-6. Footer: sticky bottom-0 bg-white border-t pt-4 px-6 pb-6 flex justify-end gap-3 — Cancel button (variant outline) + primary action button.

AvatarCluster.props: { users: Array<{ name: string; avatar?: string }>; max?: number; size?: 'sm' | 'md'; }
AvatarCluster.design: Flex row with negative margin overlap -ml-2 per avatar. Each: shadcn Avatar with ring-2 ring-white. Last item if overflow: gray circle with +N text in xs. Hover: shadcn Tooltip showing full names list. Size sm=24px md=32px.

PipelineStageBar.design: Horizontal flex of stage steps. Each step: circle icon (CheckCircle for completed, current stage circle in #9c003d, future stages gray) + label below in xs. Connected by line. Compact mode: no labels, smaller circles. Responsive: scrollable on mobile.

EmptyState.props: { icon?: React.ElementType; title: string; description?: string; action?: React.ReactNode; }
EmptyState.design: Centered py-16. Icon 48px text-gray-300. Title Syne text-lg text-gray-700. Description text-sm text-gray-400 mt-1. Action slot mt-4.

ConfirmDialog.props: { open: boolean; onOpenChange: (open: boolean) => void; title: string; description: string; confirmLabel?: string; onConfirm: () => void; variant?: 'default' | 'danger'; isLoading?: boolean; }
ConfirmDialog.design: shadcn AlertDialog. Danger variant: confirm button bg-red-600. Default variant: confirm button bg-primary. Loading state: spinner in button.

ActivityFeed.props: { events: Array<{ id: string; actorName: string; actorAvatar?: string; action: string; module: string; detail: string; timestamp: string; }>; title?: string; showAll?: boolean; }
ActivityFeed.design: Matches Image 6 Track's Actions panel. Each item: flex row — Avatar (28px) + right column (actor name font-medium + action detail text-sm text-gray-500 + module pill text-xs + timestamp text-xs text-gray-400 text-right). Items separated by subtle border-b. Last item no border.

RoleBadge.design: Uses role_badge_colors from design system. Clean pill with role label (formatted: 'Marketing Manager' not 'MARKETING_MANAGER'). Include small dot before text in same text color.

PageHeader.props: { title: string; description?: string; breadcrumbs?: Array<{ label: string; href?: string }>; actions?: React.ReactNode; }
PageHeader.design: Flex justify-between items-start mb-6. Left: breadcrumb (shadcn Breadcrumb) above title. Title Syne text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900. Description text-sm text-gray-500 mt-1. Right: actions slot (buttons).

LoadingSkeleton.variants: { table: rows x columns skeleton, card-grid: N skeleton cards, kpi-row: 4 KPI skeletons, form: labeled field skeletons }
LoadingSkeleton.design: shadcn Skeleton component with matching layouts. Animate pulse.

hooks.useSession: Client hook — reads from SessionContext, returns { session: Session | null, isLoading: boolean }
hooks.usePermission: Client hook — takes (roles: Role[]), returns boolean hasPermission. Usage: const canEdit = usePermission(['ADMISSIONS', 'MANAGING_DIRECTOR'])
hooks.useLocalStorage: Generic hook — useLocalStorage<T>(key, initialValue): [T, (value: T) => void]
hooks.useDebounce: useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay: number): T — for search input debouncing
hooks.useMediaQuery: useMediaQuery(query: string): boolean — for responsive sidebar behavior
hooks.useNotifications: Client hook — returns { notifications, unreadCount, markAsRead, markAllAsRead } from mock data filtered by session.userId
hooks.useTaskStats: Client hook — returns today's task completion stats for current user

server_actions.description: All form submissions go through Server Actions. Even though data is mocked, structure actions as real — validate with zod, return typed ActionResult, use revalidatePath to refresh data.
server_actions.pattern: 'use server' at top of each action file. Export named async functions. Each returns Promise<ActionResult>. Import mock data and simulate mutation by logging/returning modified object.

application.actions.ts: updateApplicationStatus(id: string, status: ApplicationStatus, notes: string): Promise<ActionResult>

file_structure.description: Complete Next.js 15 App Router project structure. Every file listed here must be created.

app.layout.tsx: Root layout — imports fonts (next/font/google), applies CSS variables to <html>, wraps with SessionProvider, Toaster (sonner)
app.globals.css: CSS reset, CSS variables (colors, fonts), shadcn variable overrides, scrollbar styling, utility classes

(auth).layout.tsx: Auth layout — no sidebar, full screen, bg #9c003d left brand + white right

login.LoginForm.tsx: 'use client' — the actual form component with react-hook-form

(dashboard).layout.tsx: Dashboard shell — Server Component reads session cookie, renders Sidebar + Topbar + children

dashboard.page.tsx: Server Component — detects role, renders appropriate dashboard variant

students.page.tsx: Server Component — fetches mock students, renders StudentsTable

[id].page.tsx: Server Component — fetches student by ID + all related records

actions.task.actions.ts: Server Actions for task management + EOD reports

lib.utils.ts: cn(), formatCurrency(amount, currency?), formatDate(date), formatRelativeTime(date), getPipelineStageLabel(stage), truncate(str, len)

config_files.next.config.ts: images.remotePatterns for pravatar.cc (avatar images). Experimental: typedRoutes: true for typed Next.js routing.
config_files.tailwind.config.ts: extend theme with YPIT colors, Syne + DM Sans font families, custom shadows, custom border-radius tokens
config_files.tsconfig.json: strict: true, paths aliases for @/ @/components etc.
config_files.middleware.ts: Route protection — redirect to /login if no session cookie

implementation_order.description: Strict phase-by-phase implementation guide. Do not skip phases — each builds on the previous.

phases.name: Project Setup & Foundation
tasks: 1.2: npx shadcn@latest init — style: Default, base color: Neutral, CSS variables: yes
1.3: Install all shadcn components listed in tech_stack.components.shadcn_ui.components_to_install

1.4: npm install lucide-react react-icons motion recharts react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers zod @dnd-kit/core @dnd-kit/sortable @dnd-kit/utilities date-fns sonner clsx tailwind-merge @tanstack/react-table
1.5: Configure tailwind.config.ts — add YPIT colors, Syne + DM Sans fontFamily, custom shadows and border-radius
1.6: Set up next/font/google in app/layout.tsx — import Syne and DM Sans, apply CSS variables to html element
1.7: Write app/globals.css — CSS variables for all design tokens, override shadcn primary to #9c003d HSL, scrollbar styling
1.8: Create types/index.ts with all interfaces and const objects from typescript_types section
1.9: Create lib/utils.ts with cn(), formatCurrency(), formatDate(), formatRelativeTime()
1.10: Create all lib/mock/ files with seed data
1.11: Create all zod schemas in lib/validations/
1.12: Configure next.config.ts with image domains and typedRoutes

phases.name: Authentication
tasks: 2.3: Create app/(auth)/login/LoginForm.tsx — 'use client' form with react-hook-form + zod + useActionState
2.4: Create app/(auth)/login/page.tsx — Server Component shell with brand panel
2.5: Test login flow for all 9 mock users — verify correct redirect per role
2.6: Test middleware route protection — unauthenticated users redirected to /login

phases.name: Shell Layout
tasks: 3.6: Build components/layout/Sidebar.tsx with role-based nav items, collapse behavior
3.9: Build components/layout/Topbar.tsx with search command, notifications, user dropdown
3.10: Create app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx wiring Sidebar + Topbar + main content
3.11: Test sidebar nav items per role — log in as each role and verify correct nav items appear

phases.name: Shared Components
tasks: 4.8: Build all chart components in components/charts/

phases.name: Dashboards
tasks: 5.2: Build each role dashboard component under _components/
5.4: Test each dashboard by logging in as each role

phases.name: Student Pipeline
tasks: 6.1: Build StudentsTable.tsx with all columns, sorting, pagination
6.2: Build StudentFilters.tsx with search (useDeferredValue), stage filter, country filter
6.4: Build student detail page with all tabs
6.5: Build tab components: PaymentTab, ApplicationTab, TravelTab, DocumentsTab, ActivityLogTab
6.6: Apply access_rules — hide restricted tabs per role

phases.name: Leads Management
tasks: 7.2: Build LeadsKanban.tsx with @dnd-kit drag between status columns

phases.name: Finance Module
tasks: 8.1: Build PaymentsTable.tsx matching Image 2 style exactly
8.5: Build payments page with KPI strip

phases.name: Admissions Module
tasks: 9.5: Test stage promotion: Accepted → student moves to UNIVERSITY_ACCEPTED

phases.name: Task Manager (Critical Feature)
tasks: 12.2: Build TaskCardGrid.tsx with motion stagger animation on load
12.6: Build CreateTaskPanel.tsx with multi-select assignees (shadcn Command)
12.7: Build EndOfDayReportForm.tsx with all report fields + progress slider
12.8: Build TaskDetailPanel.tsx showing task details + EOD report section
12.9: Create task.actions.ts — createTask, updateStatus, submitEndOfDayReport, acknowledgeReport
12.10: Build tasks page.tsx with tabs (My Tasks / Assigned by Me / Department / Company)
12.11: Test EOD report flow: assign task → submit report → creator acknowledges
12.12: Test role visibility: staff sees only dept tasks, MD sees all

phases.name: Staff Management
tasks: 13.2: Build AddStaffPanel.tsx with role-restricted role Select
13.6: Test IT_ADMIN cannot assign manager-level roles

phases.name: Settings & Polish
tasks: 15.2: Add loading.tsx skeletons to all routes missing them
15.3: Add error.tsx error boundaries to all route segments
15.5: Audit all forms for validation completeness
15.6: Add toast notifications (sonner) to all Server Action success/error responses
15.7: Test full user journey for all 9 roles
15.8: Responsive QA — test sidebar collapse on mobile, table horizontal scroll
15.9: Accessibility audit — check focus states, ARIA labels, keyboard nav

ai_assistant_prompt_templates.description: Ready-to-paste prompts for each major build task. Copy, paste into Cursor/Claude/Copilot, then paste the relevant JSON section.

templates.global_context_header: I am building YPIT Consultancies Internal Operations System using Next.js 15 (App Router), React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, lucide-react, react-icons, framer-motion (motion/react), recharts, react-hook-form + zod, @tanstack/react-table, @dnd-kit/core. Primary brand color: #9c003d. Fonts: Syne (headings), DM Sans (body). All data is mocked. Server Actions are used for all mutations. Use 'use client' directive ONLY when necessary. Always use TypeScript strict mode. Reference types from types/index.ts. Apply cn() from lib/utils.ts for conditional classes.
templates.phase_1_setup: TASK: Set up the Next.js 15 project foundation for YPIT-OPS. Create: (1) tailwind.config.ts with YPIT colors, Syne + DM Sans fonts, custom shadows. Requirements: (1) Login page at /login with split-screen design — left brand panel (#9c003d with YPIT logo + geometric pattern), right white form panel. (2) react-hook-form + zod validation on email + password. (3) Server Action validateLogin() that matches against mockUsers, stores session in sessionStorage + cookie, redirects to role-appropriate route. (4) middleware.ts for route protection. (5) Demo credentials expandable section on login form for all 9 roles. Reference: [paste auth + login page specs]
